L1.area: Beacon chain block processing and consensus validation logic

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where consensus-critical unsigned 64-bit integer values are represented as native JavaScript numbers, which lose precision for values greater than 2^53. This causes valid protocol messages to be incorrectly rejected or invalid messages to be incorrectly accepted due to rounding errors in large numeric comparisons, leading to a consensus split where affected nodes fork away from the main network.

L3.area: Attester slashing and proposer slashing validation within the beacon state transition layer
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where uint64 values such as slot and epoch in attester slashing and proposer slashing objects are represented as native JavaScript numbers, which lose precision for values greater than 2^53. When maliciously-crafted slashing proofs containing large uint64 fields are included on-chain, rounding errors cause valid slashing proofs to be incorrectly rejected or invalid proofs to be accepted, resulting in a consensus split where affected nodes fork away from the main network.

ground_truth.reason: Consensus-critical uint64 values (Slot, Epoch) in AttesterSlashing and ProposerSlashing objects were represented as native JavaScript numbers, which lose precision for values greater than 2^53, causing incorrect validation results and potential consensus splits.
